message:
chore(beads): close bd-2kle2 (SEC-7.3) with completion comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -444,7 +444,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ke","title":"Impl: QuickJS promise bridge + job draining semantics","description":"# Goal
Wire QuickJS async runtime to the PiJS event loop with a **correct Promise/hostcall bridge** and explicit job draining.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `host.call` returns a JS Promise backed by a Rust completion handle.
- Job draining loop: run pending jobs, then poll timers/hostcalls per spec.
- Cancellation propagation: cancel token to hostcall, reject Promise with mapped error.
- Explicit error mapping + stack traces for debugging.
- Structured logs per hostcall with correlation IDs.

# Correctness Notes
- No re-entrancy hazards: only drain jobs at safe points.
- Promise resolution order stable with scheduler sequence ids.

# Tests
- Unit tests for Promise resolution ordering.
- Conformance fixtures for cancellation/timeouts.
- E2E lifecycle harness asserts log ordering.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:13.010362542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:47.069742360Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:01:18.379160588Z","close_reason":"Implemented QuickJS promise bridge with job draining semantics. Created PiJsRuntime combining QuickJS + Scheduler + Promise bridge. Added 5 integration tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"bd-2ke","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented PiJsRuntime Promise/hostcall bridge + job draining semantics; updated json<->js conversion and added Promise bridge tests. Landed in commits: 52e33cd, 7a357f1, 0b0958a.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:07:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ki","title":"Tooling: Compatibility rewrite map + extc contract","description":"# Goal
Define the **compatibility contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
- Rewrites must be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic, semantics-preserving code patterns.
- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by branching on extension id.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Canonical rewrite map for imports + globals.
- Explicitly forbidden APIs with clear diagnostics.
- Extc input/output contract: ESM output, shim injection rules, side-effect policy.
- Compatibility matrix of supported APIs and required capabilities.
- Source map contract for accurate error locations.

# Canonical rewrite / resolution rules
## Import specifiers
Extc must ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.

Recommended canonicalization:

### A) Node builtins (`node:*`)
Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers don’t externalize them):
- `node:fs`              -> `pi:node/fs`
- `node:fs/promises`     -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `node:path`            -> `pi:node/path`
- `node:os`              -> `pi:node/os`
- `node:url`             -> `pi:node/url`
- `node:crypto`          -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `node:child_process`   -> `pi:node/child_process` (or rewrite usage to `pi.exec` when semantics preserved)
- `node:module`          -> `pi:node/module`

### B) Bare builtins (`'fs'`, `'path'`, ...)
Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these identically:
- `fs`            -> `pi:node/fs`
- `fs/promises`   -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `path`          -> `pi:node/path`
- `os`            -> `pi:node/os`
- `url`           -> `pi:node/url`
- `crypto`        -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `child_process` -> `pi:node/child_process`
- `module`        -> `pi:node/module`

## Global polyfills (injection)
Extc may inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
- `import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'` (installs `process`, `Buffer`, etc.)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'` (if needed)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'` (assume QuickJS has no wasm; installs PiWasm bridge)

Injection must be:
- deterministic (stable ordering)
- sourcemap-correct
- versioned (shim_version included in artifact hash)

# Forbidden / flagged APIs
- The contract must list:
  - **Forbidden** (hard error): APIs that bypass capability policy or escape sandbox.
  - **Flagged** (warning w/ evidence): risky constructs (dynamic eval, unbounded recursion, etc.).

Notes:
- The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for loading a bundled script; do not forbid it. Prefer “flag + log” unless we can prove safe.

# Compatibility matrix (must be sample-driven)
- The matrix must enumerate the exact Node builtins/globals required by the pinned sample set and define:
  - supported functions
  - sync vs async semantics
  - error mapping expectations
  - capability requirements + scopes

Implementation beads:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0
- node:child_process subset: bd-2sr
- WebAssembly bridge (PiWasm): bd-1ry

# Tests
- Unit transform fixtures for common imports and injection.
- Negative tests for forbidden APIs with exact messages.
- E2E harness verifies rewritten bundles run 16/16 with actionable failures.

# Dependencies
- Must align with the PiJS runtime contract + event loop semantics (bd-123) and hostcall ABI (bd-37z).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:59.736950487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:25.341887112Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:21:50.124743731Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added §2A Extc Compatibility Contract to EXTENSIONS.md with canonical rewrite rules, global polyfill injection, forbidden/flagged APIs, compatibility matrix, and sourcemap contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kj0","title":"Error UX: render hints in interactive/print/RPC modes","description":"# Goal
Surface the hint mapping to users consistently across all app modes.

# Scope
- Interactive TUI:
  - when rendering an error panel/message, include (optionally) the hint lines.
- Print mode:
  - stderr formatting includes hint lines.
- RPC mode:
  - include hints as a structured field (if protocol supports) or in a dedicated error message section.

# UX Requirements
- Hints should be visually separated from the main error message.
- Default behavior: show hints when available.
- Keep output stable for tests.

# Dependencies
- Depends on the hint mapping definition task.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one representative error per subsystem shows a hint.
- [ ] Output remains readable (no wall-of-text).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:23:10.913277673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:56.277538971Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:49:37.071270753Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kj0","depends_on_id":"bd-1iz5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kj0","depends_on_id":"bd-3am2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2kle2","title":"[SEC-7.3] Security operator handbook, runbooks, and maintenance playbooks","description":"## Background
Long-term reliability requires documentation that captures intent, operation, and incident handling.

## Scope
- Produce handbook for policy tuning, alert triage, incident handling, and postmortem workflow.
- Document maintenance procedures for scanner rules, model recalibration, and rollout controls.
- Include frequently needed troubleshooting and rollback steps.

## Deliverables
- `docs/security/` handbook set with indexed navigation.
- Runbook templates for common incident categories.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new operator can execute incident workflow without tribal knowledge.
- [ ] Docs map directly to implemented controls and commands.
- [ ] Playbooks include verification steps and expected artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:44.207486602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T12:27:57.136224186Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T12:27:57.136102249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","operations","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","depends_on_id":"bd-11mqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:06.429484640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","depends_on_id":"bd-137uj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:59:31.065797943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","depends_on_id":"bd-8lppo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:06.203038436Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1456,"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OpusAgent claiming bd-2kle2 (SEC-7.3). Will produce: (1) security operator handbook with policy tuning, alert triage, incident handling, postmortem workflow; (2) runbook templates for common incident categories; (3) maintenance procedures for scanner rules, model recalibration, rollout controls.","created_at":"2026-02-14T12:21:59Z"},{"id":1457,"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon (claude-opus-4-6) claiming bd-2kle2 (SEC-7.3). Will produce: (1) docs/security/ handbook set with indexed navigation, (2) runbook templates for common incident categories, (3) maintenance playbooks for scanner rules, model recalibration, and rollout controls.","created_at":"2026-02-14T12:22:55Z"},{"id":1458,"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-7.3 documentation complete (commit 988eb051). Three new operator runbooks:

1. docs/security/incident-response-runbook.md — Decision tree for alert classification, 5 procedural runbooks (quarantine investigation, burst denial triage, escalation review, automatic rollback verification, ledger corruption recovery), evidence collection checklist, post-incident workflow. Maps directly to ExtensionManager API and RuntimeRiskConfig.

2. docs/security/policy-tuning-guide.md — Complete parameter reference with env vars and config keys, tuning recipes for FP/FN/latency, policy profile selection guide, per-extension override patterns, graduated rollout phase progression criteria, rollback trigger thresholds, troubleshooting section.

3. docs/security/operator-quick-reference.md — Concise cheatsheet covering all env vars, rollout phases, enforcement states, risk ledger operations, kill-switch API, score band thresholds, common operations table.

Acceptance criteria met:
- New operator can execute incident workflow: 5 step-by-step procedures with verification steps
- Docs map to implemented controls: All APIs and config parameters referenced with code examples
- Playbooks include verification steps: Each procedure ends with expected artifacts and verification","created_at":"2026-02-14T12:25:25Z"},{"id":1459,"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-7.3 deliverables complete: operator-handbook.md (419 lines), incident-response-runbook.md (267 lines), policy-tuning-guide.md (208 lines), operator-quick-reference.md, security-slos.md (557 lines). 62 rollout/rollback references across 7 docs. All acceptance criteria met.","created_at":"2026-02-14T12:25:42Z"},{"id":1464,"issue_id":"bd-2kle2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-7.3 implementation complete:

1. **docs/security/operator-handbook.md** - Comprehensive security operator handbook:
   - System overview with trust boundary diagram
   - Policy configuration (profiles, precedence chain, per-extension overrides)
   - Runtime risk controller tuning guide (alpha, window, shadow mode)
   - Alert triage procedures (severity levels, categories, investigation steps)
   - Incident handling workflow (detect → contain → collect → verify → analyze → remediate)
   - Postmortem template with checklist
   - Operator command reference (all security test commands, CI gates, artifacts)

2. **docs/security/incident-runbook.md** - Step-by-step runbook for 6 incident categories:
   - INC-1: Unauthorized capability use
   - INC-2: Secret exposure
   - INC-3: Ledger integrity failure
   - INC-4: Scanner bypass
   - INC-5: Quarantine escape
   - INC-6: Resource quota abuse
   - Evidence collection procedures (bundle export, filtering, redaction, verification)
   - Rollback procedures (policy, risk controller, emergency kill-all)

3. **docs/security/maintenance-playbook.md** - Ongoing maintenance procedures:
   - Scanner rule management (adding/reviewing patterns)
   - Risk controller calibration (shadow mode, tuning, golden fixtures)
   - Policy profile updates (new caps, dangerous classification, precedence)
   - Secret broker and exec mediation maintenance
   - CI gate maintenance (thresholds, failure response)
   - Waiver lifecycle management (create, monitor, renew)
   - Extension conformance monitoring (daily dashboard, regression response)
   - Troubleshooting guide (7 common problems with solutions)

All docs map directly to implemented controls and commands. Playbooks include verification steps and expected artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-14T12:27:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2km5","title":"Reliability + failure‑mode tests","description":"# Goal
Verify extensions behave predictably under timeouts, retries, and concurrency.

# Deliverables
- Tests covering timeouts, cancellation, retries, and concurrent invocations.
- Error handling expectations per extension type.
- Logs demonstrating stable behavior (no hangs/leaks).

# Notes
These tests underpin the "super reliable" requirement.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:33:43.591005598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:38:08.870580527Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:38:08.870475411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2km5","depends_on_id":"bd-205q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:08.209803577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2km5","depends_on_id":"bd-20s9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:36:53.772822559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":353,"issue_id":"bd-2km5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Reliability issues (timeouts, retries, concurrency) are common in extension ecosystems.

Reasoning: Failure‑mode tests ensure extensions degrade gracefully and do not hang the host.

Considerations: Include cancellation paths and ensure logs capture the failure context.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:53:07Z"},{"id":626,"issue_id":"bd-2km5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 7 new reliability tests to tests/extensions_reliability.rs (total now 27 tests):

1. concurrent_event_dispatch_does_not_deadlock_or_crash - 4 threads x 25 events, 100% success
2. high_volume_error_recovery - 100 rapid throwing events, runtime survives
3. error_followed_by_success - mixed good/bad extensions, cascaded recovery
4. repeated_lifecycle_with_jittery_ext - 3 full lifecycle cycles with variable latency
5. mixed_event_types_rapid_dispatch - 80 events across 4 event types
6. shutdown_during_active_dispatch - shutdown while events still dispatching
7. load_failure_then_good_load_succeeds - syntax error load then good load works

All 70 tests pass (27 reliability + 43 common). Covers: concurrent dispatch, error recovery at scale, cascaded errors, lifecycle stability, mixed events, shutdown under contention, and load recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:37:38Z"},{"id":627,"issue_id":"bd-2km5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed extensions_reliability.rs: 27 reliability/failure-mode tests covering timeout handling, error recovery, shutdown safety, double-shutdown, load failure recovery, rapid lifecycle, concurrent dispatch, zero-timeout edge cases, large payloads, and mixed extension scenarios. All tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:38:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2knt","title":"Interactive: implement in-app /resume session picker","description":"# Goal
Implement `/resume` inside interactive mode to open the session picker UI and resume a selected session **without restarting**.

# Required Behavior
- `/resume` opens a picker overlay/component.
- Selecting a session:
  - loads the JSONL session file
  - updates agent message history to the selected leaf
  - rebuilds the conversation viewport
  - updates footer/session metadata

# Implementation Notes
- Reuse `src/session_picker.rs` where possible.
- When non-TTY, CLI flag `-r/--resume` already handles picker; this task is specifically the in-app slash command.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/resume` works from a running interactive session.
- [ ] After selection, the app behaves as if started with `--session <path>`.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
